title: pittosporum eriocarpum
intro: Pittosporum eriocarpum, also known as the New Zealand lacewood or kohuhu, is a plant species that belongs to the family Pittosporaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pittosporum eriocarpum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. Pittosporum eriocarpum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its bark, leaves, and roots are often used in traditional medicine.
